questions.q: In April 2026, Jerome Powell announced he would continue to serve as a Federal Reserve governor even after his chairmanship ends, citing what specific reason?
options: To see through an ongoing renovation probe
To oversee the rollout of a digital dollar
To launch a nationwide educational tour
To lead a transition to a new monetary standard
questions.correctText: To see through an ongoing renovation probe

questions.q: In late April 2026, prosecutors alleged that singer D4vd fatally stabbed 14-year-old Celeste Rivas Hernandez for what reason?
options: To silence her from exposing their relationship
To steal her unreleased songwriting materials
To stop her from testifying in a fraud case
To cover up a hit-and-run accident
questions.correctText: To silence her from exposing their relationship

questions.q: Ex-FBI Director James Comey surrendered to federal court in April 2026 to face charges of threatening the President's life through what type of social media post?
options: An Instagram photo of seashells arranged as '86 47'
A TikTok video of him burning a presidential seal
An X post displaying a digital painting of the White House in ruins
A Facebook live stream reading a hostile manifesto
questions.correctText: An Instagram photo of seashells arranged as '86 47'

questions.q: A 6-3 Supreme Court decision in April 2026 limited the Voting Rights Act by striking down a congressional map from which state as an unconstitutional racial gerrymander?
options: Louisiana
Texas
Alabama
Georgia

questions.q: A man from which country was sentenced to four years in prison in late April 2026 for orchestrating a massive 'swatting' spree targeting dozens of U.S. government officials?
options: Romania
Russia
North Korea
Iran
